name: postgres-execute-sql
description: Executes a SQL query against a PostgreSQL database.

name: postgres-list-tables
description: Lists PostgreSQL tables with optional filtering and detailed schema information including columns, constraints, indexes, and triggers.

table_names.description: Optional: A comma-separated list of table names. If empty, details for all tables will be listed.

output_format.description: Optional: Use 'simple' for names only or 'detailed' for full info.

name: postgres-list-views
description: Lists views in the database from pg_views with a default limit of 50 rows. Returns schemaname, viewname, ownername and the definition.

view_name.description: Optional: A specific view name to search for.

schema_name.description: Optional: A specific schema name to search for.

limit.description: Optional: The maximum number of rows to return.

name: postgres-list-schemas
description: Lists all schemas in the database ordered by schema name and excluding system and temporary schemas. It returns the schema name, schema owner, grants, number of functions, number of tables and number of views within each schema.

schema_name.description: A specific schema name pattern to search for.

owner.description: A specific schema owner name pattern to search for.

limit.description: The maximum number of schemas to return.

name: postgres-list-indexes
description: Lists available user indexes in the database, excluding system schemas (pg_catalog, information_schema).

schema_name.description: Optional: a text to filter results by schema name. The input is used within a LIKE clause.

table_name.description: Optional: a text to filter results by table name. The input is used within a LIKE clause.

index_name.description: Optional: a text to filter results by index name. The input is used within a LIKE clause.

only_unused.description: Optional: If true, only returns indexes that have never been used.

limit.description: Optional: The maximum number of rows to return. Default is 50

name: postgres-list-sequences
description: Lists sequences in the database. Returns sequence name, schema name, sequence owner, data type of the sequence, starting value, minimum value, maximum value of the sequence, the value by which the sequence is incremented, and the last value generated by the sequence in the current session.

schema_name.description: Optional: A specific schema name pattern to search for.

sequence_name.description: Optional: A specific sequence name pattern to search for.

limit.description: Optional: The maximum number of rows to return. Default is 50

name: postgres-list-stored-procedure
description: Lists stored procedure metadata including schema name, procedure name, owner, language, definition, and description.

role_name.description: The owner name to filter the stored procedures by. Defaults to NULL.

schema_name.description: The schema name to filter the stored procedures by. Defaults to NULL.

limit.description: The maximum number of stored procedures to return. Defaults to 20.

name: postgres-list-triggers
description: Lists all non-internal triggers in a database. Returns trigger name, schema name, table name, whether its enabled or disabled, timing (e.g BEFORE/AFTER of the event), the events that cause the trigger to fire such as INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE, whether the trigger activates per ROW or per STATEMENT, the handler function executed by the trigger and full definition.

trigger_name.description: Optional: A specific trigger name pattern to search for.

schema_name.description: Optional: A specific schema name pattern to search for.

table_name.description: Optional: A specific table name pattern to search for.

limit.description: Optional: The maximum number of rows to return.

name: postgres-list-pg-settings
description: Lists configuration parameters for the postgres server ordered lexicographically. Returns the parameter name, its current setting, unit of measurement, a short description, the source of the current setting, and whether a restart is required when the parameter value is changed.

setting_name.description: Optional pattern to search for a specific configuration parameter name.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of rows to return.

name: postgres-list-available-extensions
description: Lists available PostgreSQL extensions from the connected database, returning name, default_version, and description.

name: postgres-list-installed-extensions
description: Lists installed PostgreSQL extensions from the connected database.

name: postgres-list-roles
description: Lists all the user-created roles in the instance. Returns the role name, OID, max connections, along with boolean indicators for superuser status, privilege inheritance, ability to create roles/databases, login ability, replication privilege, RLS bypass, password expiration, direct members, and group memberships.

role_name.description: Filters results by role name using a LIKE clause pattern match.

limit.description: Sets the maximum number of rows returned.

name: postgres-list-locks
description: Identifies all locks held by active processes showing the process ID, user, query text, and an aggregated list of all transactions and specific locks (relation, mode, grant status) associated with each process.

name: postgres-list-tablespaces
description: Lists all tablespaces in the database, returning the tablespace name, owner name, size in bytes, internal object ID, access control list, and tablespace options.

tablespace_name.description: Optional: a text to filter results by tablespace name. The input is used within a LIKE clause.

limit.description: Optional: The maximum number of rows to return.

name: postgres-replication-stats
description: Lists each replica's process ID, user name, application name, backend_xmin, client IP address, connection state, and sync_state, along with lag sizes in bytes for sent_lag, write_lag, flush_lag, replay_lag, and total_lag.

name: postgres-list-publication-tables
description: Lists all publication tables in the database, returning publication name, schema name, and table name. Includes definition details showing what operations are replicated and the publication owner.

table_names.description: Optional: Filters results by comma-separated table names.

publication_names.description: Optional: Filters results by comma-separated publication names.

schema_names.description: Optional: Filters results by comma-separated schema names.

limit.description: Optional: Sets the maximum number of rows to return.

name: postgres-get-column-cardinality
description: Estimates the number of unique values (cardinality) quickly for one or all columns in a specific PostgreSQL table by using the database's internal statistics. Results are returned in descending order.

schema_name.description: Optional schema name where the table resides.

table_name.description: The table name containing the column to analyze.

column_name.description: Optional column name for cardinality lookup; if omitted, returns cardinality for all columns in the specified table.

name: postgres-database-overview
description: Fetches the current state of the PostgreSQL server, returning the version, whether it's a replica, uptime duration, maximum connection limit, number of current connections, number of active connections, and the percentage of connections in use.

name: postgres-list-database-stats
description: Retrieves key performance and activity statistics for each PostgreSQL database, including cache efficiency, transaction throughput, row-level activity, temporary file usage, and contention metrics.

database_name.description: Optional pattern to search for specific database names.

include_templates.description: Whether to include template databases in results.

database_owner.description: Optional pattern to search for specific database owner names.

default_tablespace.description: Optional pattern to search for specific tablespace names.

order_by.description: Field to order results by ('size' or 'commit' are valid values).

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return.

name: postgres-list-table-stats
description: Retrieves user table statistics from a PostgreSQL database ordered by sequential scans. Provides metrics including schema/table names, table sizes, scan counts, index utilization ratios, row statistics, dead row ratios, mutation counts, and vacuum operation timestamps.

schema_name.description: Filters results by a specific database schema.

table_name.description: Restricts output to a particular table.

owner.description: Narrows results to tables owned by a specific user.

limit.description: Sets the maximum number of returned rows.

name: postgres-list-active-queries
description: Lists currently active database queries from a PostgreSQL instance.

min_duration.description: Only show queries running at least this long (e.g., '1 minute', '1 second', '2 seconds').

exclude_application_names.description: A comma-separated list of application names to exclude from the query results. This is useful for filtering out queries from specific applications (e.g., 'psql', 'pgAdmin', 'DBeaver').

limit.description: The maximum number of rows to return.

name: postgres-list-query-stats
description: Lists performance statistics for executed queries ordered by total time. Requires the pg_stat_statements extension. Returns query execution metrics including timing data and buffer cache I/O statistics.

database_name.description: Optional: The database name to list query stats for.

limit.description: Optional: The maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 50.

name: postgres-long-running-transactions
description: Identifies and lists database transactions that exceed a specified time limit, providing details like process IDs, database names, usernames, application names, client addresses, connection/transaction ages, and query strings.

min_duration.description: Optional: Only show transactions running at least this long (e.g., '1 minute', '15 minutes', '30 seconds').

limit.description: Optional: The maximum number of long-running transactions to return. Defaults to 20.

name: postgres-sql
description: Executes a user-configured SQL statement against a PostgreSQL database with support for template and query parameters.

name: bigquery-execute-sql
description: Executes a SQL query against BigQuery.

dry_run.description: If set to true, the query will be validated and information about the execution will be returned without running the query.

name: bigquery-list-dataset-ids
description: Lists dataset IDs from a Google Cloud BigQuery project.

project.description: The Google Cloud project to list dataset ids.

name: bigquery-list-table-ids
description: Lists table IDs within a specified BigQuery dataset.

project.description: The Google Cloud project ID containing the dataset.

dataset.description: The dataset to list table ids.

name: bigquery-get-table-info
description: Retrieves metadata information about a BigQuery table.

project.description: The Google Cloud project ID containing the dataset and table.

table.description: The table to get metadata information.

name: bigquery-get-dataset-info
description: Retrieves metadata information about a BigQuery dataset.

project.description: The Google Cloud project ID containing the dataset.

dataset.description: The dataset to get metadata information. Can be in `project.dataset` format.

name: bigquery-search-catalog
description: Use this tool to find tables, views, models, routines or connections.

prompt.description: Prompt representing search intention. Do not rewrite the prompt.

types.description: Array of data types to filter by. Accepts: CONNECTION, POLICY, DATASET, MODEL, ROUTINE, TABLE, VIEW.

pageSize.description: Number of results in the search page.

name: bigquery-forecast
description: Enables forecasting capabilities using BigQuery's AI.FORECAST function.

history_data.description: The table ID or query containing historical time series data.

timestamp_col.description: The name of the timestamp column in the time series data.

data_col.description: The name of the data column containing the values to forecast.

id_cols.description: Column names that identify different time series within the data.

horizon.description: The number of forecast steps to generate.

name: bigquery-analyze-contribution
description: Analyzes contributions in BigQuery data using contribution analysis modeling.

input_data.description: A fully qualified BigQuery table ID or SQL query containing test and control data.

contribution_metric.description: Column name/expression for the metric to analyze; supports summable metrics, ratio metrics, and category-based metrics.

is_test_col.description: Identifies which column distinguishes test from control groups.

dimension_id_cols.description: Column names that uniquely identify each dimension.

top_k_insights_by_apriori_support.description: Number of top insights to return, ranked by apriori support.

pruning_method.description: Method for filtering insights; accepts NO_PRUNING or PRUNE_REDUNDANT_INSIGHTS.

name: bigquery-conversational-analytics
description: Enables users to query BigQuery datasets using natural language, receiving text-based analytical responses without visualizations.

user_query_with_context.description: The user's question, potentially including conversation history and system instructions for context.

table_references.description: A JSON string specifying BigQuery tables as context, where each object contains projectId, datasetId, and tableId.

name: bigquery-sql
description: Executes a user-configured SQL statement against BigQuery with support for template and query parameters.

name: spanner-execute-sql
description: Executes a SQL query against a Google Cloud Spanner database.

name: spanner-list-tables
description: Lists detailed schema information (object type, columns, constraints, indexes) as JSON for user-created tables. Filters by a comma-separated list of names. If names are omitted, lists all tables in user schemas.

table_names.description: Optional: A comma-separated list of table names. If empty, details for all tables in user-accessible schemas will be listed.

output_format.description: Optional: Use 'simple' to return table names only or use 'detailed' to return the full information schema.

name: spanner-list-graphs
description: Lists detailed graph schema information (node tables, edge tables, labels and property declarations) as JSON for user-created graphs.

graph_names.description: Optional: A comma-separated list of graph names. If empty, details for all graphs in user-accessible schemas will be listed.

output_format.description: Optional: Use 'simple' to return graph names only or use 'detailed' to return the full information schema.

name: spanner-sql
description: Executes a user-configured SQL statement against Google Cloud Spanner with support for template and query parameters.

name: mysql-execute-sql
description: Executes a SQL query against a MySQL database.

name: mysql-list-tables
description: Lists MySQL tables with optional filtering and detailed schema information including columns, constraints, indexes, and triggers.

table_names.description: Optional: A comma-separated list of table names. If empty, details for all tables will be listed.

output_format.description: Optional: Use 'simple' for names only or 'detailed' for full info.

name: mysql-get-query-plan
description: Executes a MySQL EXPLAIN query to retrieve the query execution plan in JSON format.

name: mysql-list-active-queries
description: Lists active MySQL processes and transactions, filtered by duration and limited by row count.

min_duration_secs.description: Optional: Only show queries running for at least this long in seconds.

limit.description: Optional: The maximum number of rows to return.

name: mysql-list-table-fragmentation
description: Checks all tables visible to the current user for fragmentation issues in MySQL databases.

table_schema.description: The database where fragmentation check is to be executed.

table_name.description: Name of the table to be checked.

data_free_threshold_bytes.description: Only show tables with at least this much free space in bytes.

limit.description: Max rows to return.

name: mysql-list-tables-missing-unique-indexes
description: Identifies MySQL tables that lack primary key or unique index constraints, excluding system databases.

table_schema.description: The database where the check is to be performed. Check all tables visible to the current user if not specified.

limit.description: Max rows to return, default is 50.

name: mysql-sql
description: Executes a user-configured SQL statement against a MySQL database with support for template and query parameters.

name: snowflake-execute-sql
description: Executes a SQL query against a Snowflake database.

name: snowflake-sql
description: Executes a user-configured SQL statement against Snowflake with support for template and query parameters.

name: neo4j-execute-cypher
description: Executes Cypher queries against Neo4j databases.

dry_run.description: When enabled, the query undergoes validation and execution details are provided without actually running it.

name: neo4j-schema
description: Extracts and provides comprehensive schema information from Neo4j databases, including node labels, relationships, constraints, and indexes.

name: neo4j-cypher
description: Executes a user-configured Cypher statement against Neo4j with support for parameterized queries.

name: redis
description: Executes commands against a Redis data store. Parameters are dynamically defined per tool instance via YAML configuration.

name: bigtable-sql
description: Executes a SQL statement against a BigTable data source with support for template and standard parameters.

name: elasticsearch-esql
description: Executes SQL queries against Elasticsearch using the ES|QL query language.

name: alloydb-ai-nl
description: Executes natural language queries against AlloyDB using the alloydb_ai_nl.execute_nl_query() function.

question.description: The natural language question to ask.

name: alloydb-list-clusters
description: Lists all AlloyDB clusters in a given project and location.

project.description: The GCP project ID to list clusters for.

location.description: Optional: The geographic region (e.g., 'us-central1'). Defaults to '-' which lists clusters across all locations.

name: alloydb-get-cluster
description: Retrieves details about a specific AlloyDB cluster.

location.description: The geographic location where the cluster resides (e.g., 'us-central1').

cluster.description: The unique identifier for the cluster.

name: alloydb-create-cluster
description: Creates a new AlloyDB cluster. This is a long-running operation, but the API call returns quickly. Returns operation id to be used by the wait-for-operation tool.

cluster.description: A unique identifier for the AlloyDB cluster.

password.description: Secure password for the initial database user.

network.description: VPC network name to connect the cluster to.

name: alloydb-create-instance
description: Creates a new AlloyDB instance (PRIMARY or READ_POOL) within a cluster. This is a long-running operation. Returns operation id to be used by the wait-for-operation tool.

cluster.description: The cluster ID where the instance will be created.

instance.description: A unique identifier for the new AlloyDB instance.

instanceType.description: Instance category; accepts PRIMARY or READ_POOL.

displayName.description: An optional user-friendly name for the instance.

nodeCount.description: Number of nodes in the read pool (required for READ_POOL type).

name: alloydb-list-instances
description: Lists all AlloyDB instances in a given project, location and cluster.

location.description: Optional: The cluster location (e.g., 'us-central1'). Defaults to '-' for all regions.

cluster.description: Optional: The cluster identifier. Defaults to '-' to retrieve instances from all clusters.

name: alloydb-get-instance
description: Retrieves details about a specific AlloyDB instance.

name: alloydb-create-user
description: Creates a new AlloyDB user within a cluster. Takes the new user's name and a secure password. Optionally, a list of database roles can be assigned.

cluster.description: The cluster identifier where the user will be created.

password.description: A secure password required only for ALLOYDB_BUILT_IN user types.

databaseRoles.description: Optional list of database roles to assign (e.g., 'pg_read_all_data').

userType.description: The user category: either ALLOYDB_BUILT_IN or ALLOYDB_IAM_USER.

name: alloydb-get-user
description: Retrieves details about a specific AlloyDB user.

name: alloydb-list-users
description: Lists all AlloyDB users in a given project, location and cluster.

cluster.description: The ID of the cluster from which users will be listed.

name: alloydb-wait-for-operation
description: Polls the AlloyDB operations API until the operation is done. Once the instance is created, provides follow-up steps on how to use the variables to bring the data plane MCP server up in local and remote setup.

location.description: The location ID where the operation is running.

operation.description: The operation ID to monitor for completion.

name: cloud-sql-list-instances
description: Lists all types of Cloud SQL instances for a project.

name: cloud-sql-get-instance
description: Gets a particular Cloud SQL instance.

instanceId.description: The instance ID that identifies which Cloud SQL instance to retrieve.

name: cloud-sql-create-database
description: Creates a new database in a Cloud SQL instance.

instance.description: The ID of the instance where the database will be created.

name.description: The name for the new database. Must be unique within the instance.

name: cloud-sql-list-databases
description: Lists all databases for a Cloud SQL instance.

name: cloud-sql-clone-instance
description: Clones an existing Cloud SQL instance into a new instance. The clone can be a direct copy of the source instance, or a point-in-time-recovery (PITR) clone from a specific timestamp. Call the wait_for_operation tool after this with multiplier=4.

sourceInstanceName.description: The name of the instance to be cloned.

destinationInstanceName.description: The name of the new instance being created via cloning.

pointInTime.description: RFC 3339 formatted timestamp for point-in-time recovery (optional).

preferredZone.description: Desired zone location for the new instance (optional).

preferredSecondaryZone.description: Desired secondary zone for the new instance (optional).

name: cloud-sql-create-backup
description: Creates a backup on a Cloud SQL instance.

instance.description: Cloud SQL instance ID, excluding the project ID.

name: cloud-sql-create-users
description: Creates a new user in a Cloud SQL instance. Both built-in and IAM users are supported. IAM users require an email account as the user name.

instance.description: The ID of the instance where the user will be created.

name.description: The name for the new user.

password.description: A secure password for the new user (not required for IAM users).

iamUser.description: Set to true to create a Cloud IAM user.

name: cloud-sql-restore-backup
description: Restores a backup on a Cloud SQL instance.

target_instance.description: Cloud SQL instance identifier for the destination.

backup_id.description: Identifier for the backup being restored, accepting BackupRun IDs, backup names, or BackupDR backup names.

source_project.description: GCP project ID where the backup originates (conditionally required for BackupRun IDs).

source_instance.description: Cloud SQL instance identifier where the backup originates (conditionally required for BackupRun IDs).

name: cloud-sql-wait-for-operation
description: Polls the Cloud SQL operations API until the operation is done. Provides follow-up steps once completed.

name: firestore-add-documents
description: Adds documents to a Firestore collection.

collectionPath.description: The relative path for the collection where documents will be added, such as 'users' or 'users/userId/posts'.

documentData.description: The document data in Firestore's native JSON format. Each field must be wrapped with a type indicator.

returnData.description: When enabled, the response includes the created document's data.

name: firestore-get-documents
description: Retrieves documents from Firestore based on provided relative document paths.

documentPaths.description: Array of relative document paths to retrieve from Firestore (e.g., 'users/userId' or 'users/userId/posts/postId').

name: firestore-delete-documents
description: Deletes documents from Firestore based on provided relative document paths.

documentPaths.description: Array of relative document paths to delete from Firestore (e.g., 'users/userId' or 'users/userId/posts/postId').

name: firestore-get-rules
description: Retrieves Firebase Firestore security rules.

name: firestore-list-collections
description: Lists collections from Firestore, either at the root level or from a specified parent document path.

parentPath.description: Relative parent document path to list subcollections from (e.g., 'users/userId'). If not provided, lists root collections.

name: firestore-query
description: Executes queries against Firestore collections with support for filtering, ordering, field selection, and result limiting.

collectionPath.description: The Firestore collection path to query.

filters.description: Query filters in simplified JSON format supporting AND/OR logic and operators like ==, !=, <, >, <=, >=, array-contains, in, not-in.

select.description: Array of field names to retrieve.

orderBy.description: Configuration object with 'field' and 'direction' (ASCENDING/DESCENDING) for sorting.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

name: firestore-query-collection
description: Queries a Firestore collection with filtering, ordering, and analysis capabilities.

collectionPath.description: The relative path to the Firestore collection to query (e.g., 'users' or 'users/userId/posts').

filters.description: Array of filter objects with field, operator, and value properties.

orderBy.description: JSON string specifying the field and direction to order by (e.g., {"field": "name", "direction": "ASCENDING"}).

limit.description: The maximum number of documents to return from the query.

analyzeQuery.description: If true, returns query explain metrics including execution statistics.

name: firestore-update-document
description: Updates documents in Firestore with selective field control and optional data retrieval.

documentPath.description: The relative path of the document to update (e.g., 'users/userId').

documentData.description: Document data in Firestore's native JSON format with type indicators.

updateMask.description: The selective fields to update. If not provided, all fields in documentData will be updated. Supports dot notation for nested fields.

returnData.description: If set to true the output will have the data of the updated document.

name: firestore-validate-rules
description: Validates Firestore Rules source code.

source.description: The Firestore Rules source code to validate.

name: mongodb-find
description: Queries multiple documents in a MongoDB collection with support for filter, projection, and sort parameters.

name: mongodb-find-one
description: Retrieves a single document from a MongoDB collection.

name: mongodb-insert-one
description: Inserts a single document into a MongoDB collection.

data.description: The JSON payload to insert, should be a JSON object.

name: mongodb-insert-many
description: Performs bulk insertion of multiple documents into a MongoDB collection.

name: mongodb-update-one
description: Updates a single document in a MongoDB collection based on filter criteria.

name: mongodb-update-many
description: Performs batch updates on multiple documents in a MongoDB collection.

name: mongodb-delete-one
description: Deletes a single document from a MongoDB collection based on filter criteria.

name: mongodb-delete-many
description: Performs batch deletion of multiple documents from a MongoDB collection.

name: mongodb-aggregate
description: Executes an aggregation pipeline against a MongoDB collection.

name: dataproc-list-clusters
description: Lists and filters Dataproc clusters.

filter.description: Constrains clusters to list using case-sensitive syntax: `field = value [AND [field = value]]...`. Supported fields: status.state, clusterName, labels.[KEY].

pageSize.description: Maximum number of clusters returned in a single response page.

pageToken.description: Pagination token received from previous ListClusters call.

name: dataproc-get-cluster
description: Gets a Dataproc cluster.

clusterName.description: The short name of the cluster, e.g. for 'projects/my-project/regions/us-central1/clusters/my-cluster', pass 'my-cluster'.

name: dataproc-list-jobs
description: Lists and filters Dataproc jobs.

filter.description: Constrains jobs to list using case-sensitive syntax with field operators like clusterName, status.state, or labels.[KEY].

jobStateMatcher.description: Specifies matching scope: ALL, ACTIVE, or NON_ACTIVE jobs.

pageSize.description: Sets the maximum number of jobs returned per page.

pageToken.description: Pagination token from previous ListJobs calls.

name: dataproc-get-job
description: Gets a Dataproc job.

jobId.description: The job ID, e.g. for 'projects/my-project/regions/us-central1/jobs/my-job', pass 'my-job'.

name: dataplex-search-entries
description: Searches Dataplex entries matching a query.

query.description: The query against which entries in scope should be matched.

pageSize.description: Number of results in the search page.

orderBy.description: Specifies the ordering of results. Supported values are: relevance, last_modified_timestamp, last_modified_timestamp asc.

name: dataplex-lookup-entry
description: Looks up a specific Dataplex entry by resource name.

name.description: The project to which the request should be attributed in the following form: projects/{project}/locations/{location}.

entry.description: The resource name of the Entry in the following form: projects/{project}/locations/{location}/entryGroups/{entryGroup}/entries/{entry}.

view.description: Specifies which parts of the entry and aspects to return. Options: 1 (BASIC), 2 (FULL), 3 (CUSTOM), or 4 (ALL).

aspectTypes.description: Limits the aspects returned to the provided aspect types. Works together with CUSTOM view.

name: dataplex-search-aspect-types
description: Searches Dataplex aspect types matching a query.

query.description: The query against which aspect types should be matched.

pageSize.description: Number of returned aspect types in the search page.

orderBy.description: Specifies the ordering of results. Supported values are: relevance, last_modified_timestamp, last_modified_timestamp asc.

name: cloud-logging-admin-query-logs
description: Queries Cloud Logging logs using filter expressions and returns matching entries with optional verbosity settings.

filter.description: Cloud Logging filter query. Common fields: resource.type, resource.labels.*, logName, severity, textPayload, jsonPayload.*, protoPayload.*, labels.*, httpRequest.*

newestFirst.description: Set to true for newest logs first. Defaults to oldest first.

startTime.description: Start time in RFC3339 format; defaults to 30 days prior.

endTime.description: End time in RFC3339 format (e.g., 2025-12-09T23:59:59Z). Defaults to now.

verbose.description: Include additional fields (insertId, trace, spanId, httpRequest, labels, operation, sourceLocation).

limit.description: Maximum log entries to return.

name: cloud-logging-admin-list-log-names
description: Lists available log names in Cloud Logging.

limit.description: Maximum number of log entries to return. Default: 200.

name: cloud-logging-admin-list-resource-types
description: Lists resource types available through Cloud Logging.

name: cloud-monitoring-query-prometheus
description: Executes a PromQL query against Google Cloud Monitoring.

projectId.description: The Id of the Google Cloud project.

name: wait
description: Pauses execution for a specified duration.

duration.description: The duration to wait for, specified as a string (e.g., '10s', '2m', '1h').

name: http
description: Makes an HTTP request to a configured endpoint. Supports path params, query params, body params, and header params. Tool name, description, method, and path are defined in YAML configuration.

name: looker-query
description: Executes a Looker query against a model and explore, returning results as JSON.

pivots.description: The query pivots (must be included in fields as well).

limit.description: The row limit.

name: looker-query-sql
description: Executes a Looker inline SQL query and returns results.

limit.description: The row limit.

name: looker-query-url
description: Creates a Looker query URL that can be shared.

limit.description: The row limit.

vis_config.description: The visualization config for the query.

name: looker-get-explores
description: Retrieves explores from a specified Looker model.

name: looker-get-dimensions
description: Retrieves dimension fields from a specified Looker model and explore.

name: looker-get-measures
description: Retrieves measure fields from a specified Looker model and explore.

name: looker-get-filters
description: Retrieves filter fields from a specified Looker model and explore.

name: looker-get-parameters
description: Retrieves parameter fields from a specified Looker model and explore.

name: looker-get-dashboards
description: Searches and retrieves dashboards from a Looker instance.

limit.description: The number of dashboards to fetch. Default 100.

offset.description: The number of dashboards to skip before fetching. Default 0.

name: looker-get-looks
description: Retrieves looks (saved queries) from a Looker instance with optional filtering.

desc.description: Filter looks based on their description.

limit.description: The number of looks to fetch. Default 100.

offset.description: The number of looks to skip before fetching. Default 0.

name: looker-get-models
description: Retrieves LookML models from a Looker instance.

name: looker-run-look
description: Runs a Looker look (saved query) and returns the results as JSON data.

look_id.description: The id of the look to run.

limit.description: The row limit. Default 500.

name: looker-run-dashboard
description: Executes a Looker dashboard and returns its results.

dashboard_id.description: The id of the dashboard to run.

name: looker-make-dashboard
description: Creates a new Looker dashboard.

folder.description: The folder id where the Dashboard will be created. Leave blank to use the user's personal folder.

name: looker-make-look
description: Creates a new Looker look (saved query with visualization).

folder.description: The folder id where the Look will be created. Leave blank to use the user's personal folder.

vis_config.description: The visualization config for the query.

limit.description: The row limit.

name: looker-add-dashboard-element
description: Adds a new tile (dashboard element) to a Looker dashboard.

dashboard_id.description: The id of the dashboard where this tile will exist.

title.description: The title of the Dashboard Element.

vis_config.description: The visualization config for the query.

dashboard_filters.description: Array of filter objects with dashboard_filter_name and field properties to link dashboard filters to the query.

limit.description: The row limit.

name: looker-add-dashboard-filter
description: Adds a filter to a Looker dashboard.

dashboard_id.description: The id of the dashboard where this filter will exist.

name.description: The name of the Dashboard Filter.

title.description: The title of the Dashboard Filter.

default_value.description: The default_value of the Dashboard Filter (optional).

allow_multiple_values.description: Whether the filter permits multiple selections.

required.description: Whether dashboard execution requires this filter.

name: looker-conversational-analytics
description: Enables natural language queries against Looker explores through Google's Gemini Data Analytics API, returning analysis in plain text.

user_query_with_context.description: The user's question, potentially including conversation history and system instructions for context.

explore_references.description: Array of 1-5 explore references. Each reference is a map containing 'model' (MODEL_NAME) and 'explore' (EXPLORE_NAME).

name: looker-generate-embed-url
description: Generates an embed URL for Looker content (dashboards, looks, or query visualizations).

type.description: The category of Looker content to embed (e.g., dashboards, looks, query-visualization).

id.description: The identifier for the specific content to embed.

name: looker-get-projects
description: Retrieves all LookML projects from a Looker instance.

name: looker-validate-project
description: Validates a LookML project in Looker.

project_id.description: The id of the project to validate.

name: looker-get-project-files
description: Retrieves file listings from a Looker project.

project_id.description: The id of the project containing the files.

name: looker-get-project-file
description: Retrieves the content of a specific file from a Looker project.

project_id.description: The id of the project containing the files.

file_path.description: The path of the file within the project.

name: looker-create-project-file
description: Creates a new file within a Looker project.

project_id.description: The id of the project containing the files.

file_path.description: The path of the file within the project.

name: looker-update-project-file
description: Updates the content of an existing file within a Looker project.

project_id.description: The id of the project containing the files.

file_path.description: The path of the file within the project.

name: looker-delete-project-file
description: Deletes a file from a Looker project.

project_id.description: The id of the project containing the files.

file_path.description: The path of the file within the project.

name: looker-get-project-directories
description: Retrieves directory structure from a Looker project.

name: looker-create-project-directory
description: Creates a new directory within a Looker project.

directory_path.description: The path to create in the project.

name: looker-delete-project-directory
description: Deletes a directory from a Looker project.

directory_path.description: The path to delete in the project.

name: looker-get-connections
description: Retrieves all database connections configured in Looker.

name: looker-get-connection-databases
description: Retrieves available databases for a specific Looker connection.

name: looker-get-connection-schemas
description: Retrieves schema information from a Looker connection.

name: looker-get-connection-tables
description: Retrieves table metadata from a Looker connection.

name: looker-get-connection-table-columns
description: Retrieves column metadata from specified database tables within a Looker connection.

tables.description: A comma separated list of tables containing the columns.

name: looker-dev-mode
description: Toggles Looker development mode on or off.

name: looker-health-analyze
description: Runs health analysis on Looker projects, models, or explores.

action.description: The analysis to run. Can be 'projects', 'models', or 'explores'.

timeframe.description: The timeframe in days to analyze.

min_queries.description: The minimum number of queries for a model or explore to be considered used.

name: looker-health-pulse
description: Runs diagnostic health checks on a Looker instance.

action.description: The health check to run. Can be: check_db_connections, check_dashboard_performance, check_dashboard_errors, check_explore_performance, check_schedule_failures, check_legacy_features.

name: looker-health-vacuum
description: Analyzes Looker usage patterns to identify unused models, explores, fields, and joins.

action.description: The vacuum operation to execute; accepts either 'models' or 'explores'.

project.description: Designates which Looker project to analyze.

model.description: Specifies the Looker model for analysis.

name: looker-get-lookml-tests
description: Retrieves LookML tests for a specified Looker project.

project_id.description: The unique ID of the LookML project.

file_id.description: Optional ID of the file to filter tests by. Must be the complete file path from the project root (e.g., 'models/my_model.model.lkml').

name: looker-run-lookml-tests
description: Runs LookML tests for a specified Looker project.

project_id.description: The id of the project to run LookML tests for.

file_id.description: Optional id of the file to run tests for.

test.description: Optional name of the test to run.

model.description: Optional name of the model to run tests for.

name: looker-create-view-from-table
description: Generates LookML view files for specified database tables in a Looker project.

project_id.description: The id of the project to create the view in.

tables.description: The tables to generate views for. Each object requires: schema (string), table_name (string), and optionally: primary_key (string), base_view (boolean), columns (array of objects with column_name).

folder_name.description: The folder to place the view files in (e.g., 'views').
